methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $35M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $400M - $2.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 64.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: ClearSpace

data.narrative:
Array Labs, a Series A space and aerospace company founded by Andrew Peterson and Jose Isaac Robledo, has raised $35M to date. Valuation analysis draws on two applicable triangulation methods anchored to a comparable universe of 4 private peers at same or adjacent stage (ClearSpace, Cowboy Space Corporation, Aalyria, Hubble Network) and 5 public space-sector comps (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x multiplier to the $35M raised proxy, yielding $70M. The peer-median method across the 4 same-sector private comparables—spanning a range of $400M to $2.0B—implies a $850M median valuation. Public-comp anchoring is not applicable due to lack of disclosed revenue; the Space & Aerospace sector median P/S of 64.2x cannot be applied without the target's top line.

The two applicable methods produce a wide range. The low end anchors near $60M (conservative stage-progression), while the high end reaches $977M (peer median plus buffer). The median implied valuation across methods is $460M. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory, unit economics, and whether Array Labs' technology and traction warrant positioning toward the upper or lower quartile of the peer set. Additional disclosure of revenue run rate or customer concentration would materially sharpen the range.
